original: This fuzzy red hat looks straight out of Terran Christmas tales of yore. It comes complete with luxurious white fur at the base and an orange sized ball of alabaster fluff at the very apex. The craftsmanship is impeccable. This is no ten credit costume piece. It’s a high-quality piece of haberdashery fit for the big red man himself - or a daring space captain who found a place in [pc.hisHer] heart to help out a poor lost soul during the holidays. Just looking at it makes you feel warm and fuzzy all over.

original: Kor’diiak hide is renowned for its durability and resistance to cold. Unlike many other materials, it does not stiffen or become brittle in sub-freezing temperatures, nor does it require special treatment to prevent rot.\n\nThis particular suit is an absolute masterwork. The stitching is near-perfect. Individual frostwyrm scales decorate the chest and reinforce potential weakpoints. The lining is thick and soft, made from some Uvetan beast you’re not quite familiar with. It’s quite the gift.
context: <<POS:22505670>> or’diiak hide armor",e.tooltip='Kor’diiak hide is renowned for its durability and resistance'

original: This mirrored jumpsuit is branded as a “Refrax 4 Tacsuit.” You can see where it gets the name - the entire suit is massively reflective. original: Touching a small stud on the collar, you command the Omnisuit to retract. It does so at once, making you shiver and shudder as it disengages from your [pc.skinFurScales]. The crawling latex tickles at first, but with each blob that flows up into the collar, the sensations deaden. Once you’re completely uncovered, the collar hisses and snaps open, falling into a numbed palm. Your sense of touch is vastly diminished without the suit, leading you to wonder if it wouldn’t be better to just put it back on.
